english: been fried, greaves; gál-ro (gal ro), rdó-ro (rdo ro), sá-ro (sa ro) rubbish; skúd-ro (skud ro) the ends of threads in a seam; v. also ro-tó (ro to). Comp. ro-káṅ (ro khang), col. *rom-kaṅ* (*rom khang*) place for burning or burying the dead, a favourite spot for conjurations and sorceries. — ro-grib (ro grib) defilement by contact with dead bodies. — ro-rgyáb (ro rgyab) back, back part Lt. — ro-sgám (ro sgam) coffin. — ro-tó (ro to) Ld. (= ro II, 3) residue; *raṅ-sii ro-to* (*rang sii ro to*) wax; *śig-pe ro-tó* (*shig pe ro to*) ruins of walls. — ro-stód (ro stod) the upper part of the human body, chest and back Stg.; esp. back Mil. — ro-dóm (ro dom) fees given to the Lamas for performing the burial or cremation ceremonies Mil. — ro-búg (ro bug) Sch. grave, tomb. — ro-myágs (ro myags) v. myags (myags) — ro-smád (ro smad) the lower part of the body Med., ro-smád sbrúl-du ḳyil-ba (ro smad sbrul du 'khyil ba) the lower part of the body like a winding serpent Wdk. — ro-rás (ro ras) cloth of cotton for wrapping up a dead body before cremation; upon it incantations are frequently written against demons and malignant spirits Pth. — ro-laṅs (ro langs) = वेताल (evil) spirit, or goblin that occupies a dead body (Will.) Tar. 158. — ro-śiṅ (ro shing) wood for burning a dead body.
Stg. = ra-nyé (ra nye), źa-nyé (zha nye) lead.
1. sometimes for ro (ro) Cs., Schr. — 2. v. rtsa I (rtsa I).

sexual instinct, carnal desire, lust Med., ró-tsa skyéd-pa (ro tsa skyed pa) to excite, to increase the carnal appetite by medicine Cs.; also: to feel it; ró-tsa-ba (ro tsa ba) 1. voluptuous, sensual, lustful Mil. 2. exciting or animating the sexual instinct Wdṅ.

1. C. black, cf. bya- (bya-) and po-róg (pho rog). — 2. W.=rág-pa (rag pa) reddish, yellow-ish-brown, of rocks. — róg-ge-ba (rog ge ba) shining dimly; źal ºdzum-nág róg-ge-ba (zhal 'dzum nag rog ge ba) with a face glowing gloomily as it were Mil. nt. — rog-róg (rog rog) 1. C. jet-black. 2. 'dark-grey' Sch., prob. = róg-po (rog po) 2. — 3. rogue, villain Cs. (a man of dark deeds?).

vulgar pronunciation of grogs (grogs), friend, companion, associate, assistant v. grogs (grogs); rogs-méd gćig-pa (rogs med gcig pa) quite alone Pth.; *roy-rám ċó-ċe* (*roy ram co ce*) W. = ra-mda byéd-pa (ra mda' byed pa); *rógs-po* (*rogs po*) Ld. adulterer, *róg-po ċó-ċe* (*rog po co ce*) (of a husband) and *róg-mo ċó-ċe* (*rog mo co ce*) (of a wife) to commit adultery.

narrow passage, defile, cleft in a hill, also valley; brag-róṅ (brag rong) dell or chasm be-tween rocks, ravine, roṅ-rtsúb (rong rtsub) a rough country full of ravines, so Tibet is called Glr.; róṅ-yul (rong yul) id.; róṅ-mi (rong mi), róṅ-rta (rong rta), róṅ-lćaṅ (rong lcang) a man coming from, a horse bred in, a willow grow-ing in such a country.
stiff, unable to help one's self, ród-lći-ba (rod lci ba) Sch. id.; Ld.: *rod-da-rod-de* (*rod da rod de*) of decrepit or sick people.

W (for sbóm-po (sbom po) C., B.) thick, big, stout, of men, trees, sticks; mas-sive, massy, plump; deep, of sounds, opp. to ṕrá-mo (phra mo). — róm-yig (rom yig) type, types, letters used in printing, opp. to ṕra-yig (phra yig), v. yi-ge (yi ge).

1. side, only in the comp.: náṅ-rol (nang rol) inside, ṕyi-rol (phyi rol) outside, ṕá-rol (pha rol), tsú-rol (tshu rol) etc.; mál-gyi ṕyi-rol (mal gyi phyi rol) the outside of the bed (e.g. has been soiled) Glr.; mostly as postposition: yáṅs-pa-ċan-gyi náṅ-rol-na (yangs pa can gyi nang rol na) within the town of Yaṅ-pa-ċan (Yang pa can); náṅ-rol-nas ºbúl-ba (nang rol nas 'bul ba) to reach, to hand from within Dzl.; ćui ṕá-rol-na (chu'i pha rol na), tsú-rol-na (tshu rol na) (or tsú-rol-tu (tshu rol tu)) on the other side or on this side of the water; gyás-rol (g.yas rol), gyón-rol (g.yon rol) the right side, the left side; also in a looser sense: ṕyi-rol-tu bzuṅ-ba (phyi rol tu bzung ba) to look upon a thing as externally or really existing Mil.; often pleon.: sṅón-rol-nas (sngon rol nas) before, previously Thgy.; ºóg-rol-tu ('og rol tu) for ºóg-tu ('og tu) after Pth., Tar.; ºdi-nas nyi-ma-núb-kyi pyógs-rol-na ('di nas nyi ma nub kyi phyogs rol na) to the west from here. — 2. Sch.: rol(-tu) bsád-pato (rol (-tu) bsad pa to) destroy completely, to kill on the spot(?). — 3. (Cs. also rol-mo (rol mo)) furrow; rol rmód-pa (rol rmod pa) to make furrows, to plough.
Sch.: the near horse in a team, the right-hand horse.
1. to amuse or divert one's self (synon. with rtsé-ba (rtse ba)), thus one of the twelve actions of a Buddha is btsun-moi ºkór- (btsun mo'i 'khor-)
